100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 26, 1926, the ongoing tensions in the Middle East were underscored by reports of violent clashes between Arab and Jewish communities in Palestine, raising concerns over stability in the region. Meanwhile, in the United States, the Supreme Court upheld the constitutionality of the National Industrial Recovery Act, a key piece of President Franklin D. Roosevelt's New Deal legislation aimed at economic recovery. Additionally, the world of sports buzzed with excitement as the baseball season progressed, with the New York Yankees continuing to dominate the American League standings.
Updated: Friday, June 26, 2026 at 08:51:37 AM CDT
